safetyZones.notes: Central Delhi hub. Busy and well-patrolled during the day. After midnight, the outer circle gets deserted — avoid walking alone.

safetyZones.area: South Delhi (GK, Defence Colony, Hauz Khas)
safetyZones.notes: Affluent residential area. Well-lit, good police presence. Hauz Khas Village gets crowded at night with bar-goers.
Major shopping area, extremely crowded. Pickpocketing risk. Narrow lanes can feel claustrophobic. Avoid after dark.
Backpacker area near New Delhi station. High scam risk, aggressive touts. Solo travelers especially women should be cautious.
Very crowded, narrow lanes, cycle rickshaws. Amazing food but keep valuables hidden. Not safe to walk after 9 PM.
Airport is well-secured. Use official taxi counter or Ola/Uber. Avoid random cab offers outside terminal.
Planned residential area with metro connectivity. Generally safe. Some sectors feel empty at night.
IT sector is well-lit. Sector 18 for shopping/entertainment. Outskirts and construction zones — avoid at night.
Cyber City area is safe and well-patrolled. MG Road nightlife area — use cabs. Outer areas have poor infrastructure.
High scam zone. Fake travel agents, overpriced porters, cab touts. Walk directly to the official prepaid taxi stand.

fareBenchmarks.notes: Ola/Uber 400-550. Delhi Meru cab 500-700. Prepaid taxi at airport counter ~600.
25-40 min depending on traffic. NH48 can be jammed during rush hours.
45-75 min. Route goes through central Delhi. Late night is faster.
2 km, 10 min. Autos will quote 150+. Walk or use metro (1 stop).
Delhi Metro is the best transit option. Token fare ₹10-60. Smart card recommended.
Meter rate: ₹25 for first 1.5 km, then ₹8/km. Most drivers refuse meter — negotiate hard.

scamAlerts.description: Fake 'Government Tourism Office' shops near the station. They'll rebook your hotel or train at inflated prices. Only use tourism.gov.in or the official counter inside the station.
Taxi/auto touts outside the terminal quote 3-5x. Use the official prepaid taxi counter inside arrivals, or book Ola/Uber from the designated pickup zone.
Someone 'befriends' you and takes you to a shop to buy gems/carpets to 'export for profit'. It's a scam — the goods are worthless.
Drivers/touts say your destination is 'closed today' and offer alternatives (their commission spots). Always verify independently — call the place or check Google.
Some auto meters run fast (rigged). Compare with Google Maps estimated fare. If the fare seems absurd, note the auto number and report to 1095.

localTips: Delhi Metro is your best friend — clean, cheap, safe, and covers most tourist areas
Download the DMRC app for metro routes and timings
Carry a scarf/mask — air quality can be poor, especially Oct-Feb
Street food is incredible but stick to busy stalls with high turnover. Avoid pre-cut fruit.
Peak traffic: 8-10 AM and 5-8 PM. The city genuinely gridlocks. Plan accordingly.
Hindi is primary but English works in South Delhi, airports, and hotels
Uber/Ola work well. Always share your ride status with someone you trust.
